structural_claim: Gauge redundancy in physical theories requires the introduction of unphysical degrees of freedom whose careful treatment is essential for maintaining consistency

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Gauge fixing introduces fictitious anticommuting fields that cancel unphysical degrees of freedom to maintain unitarity
Gauge theories possess hidden fermionic symmetry encoding gauge invariance after gauge fixing
Quantum gravity path integral requires summing over metrics modulo diffeomorphisms, with fundamental ambiguities in the measure
Holographic encoding implements redundancy where bulk operators are protected against boundary erasures
